danger_signals.visual_form: Jail UI element in player status display
danger_signals.meaning: Player movement restricted; financial penalty or strategic disadvantage

danger_signals.visual_form: Currency depletion display; player elimination from game
danger_signals.meaning: Player financial failure; loss of property and game elimination

danger_signals.visual_form: Color-coded property squares on board; ownership markers
danger_signals.meaning: Territory control by opponents; financial liability (rent payments)

safety_signals.visual_form: Color-matched property display indicating player ownership
safety_signals.meaning: Secure asset; income generation; territorial control

safety_signals.visual_form: Currency value shown in player status/bank display
safety_signals.meaning: Financial stability; ability to pay obligations and acquire properties
No financial threat; temporary reprieve from economic pressure

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct color palette per player (e.g., red, blue, yellow, green, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character tokens/avatars (Rich Uncle Pennybags portrait for human player)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Trade Screen character portraits with three emotional states: neutral, happy, angry face animations (Japanese version feature)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Property color groups matching player ownership; board position indicator

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Portrait with white outline (tweaked in Japanese version)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Title screen presence; represents game rules enforcement and economic system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Board shows empty/neutral properties; multiple color groups unclaimed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players actively bidding and purchasing; frequent property transactions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Board displays color-grouped properties increasingly consolidated by players; property color patterns emerge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rent collection events; trading negotiations between players

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Few unclaimed properties; most board spaces display ownership; visual concentration of color groups
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased bankruptcy risk; higher rent payments; player elimination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI displays 'IN JAIL' status; inverse of 'YOU'RE NOT IN JAIL' message
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Restricted movement; strategic decision to remain in jail vs. pay bail

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: German version: Deutsche mark symbol, values multiplied by 20; French version: franc symbol, values multiplied by 100; Large coin pile on victory screen replaces dollar sign
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI trading behavior affected (German version oversight: AI uses original English prices creating exploitable trades)

environmental_storytelling.example: Property squares display player-matched colors, creating visual narrative of territorial expansion and monopoly formation
Player status displays (cash reserve, properties owned) communicate relative economic power and survival probability
Trade screen animated portraits showing emotional states (neutral/happy/angry) reflect NPC attitude toward proposed trades, indicating AI valuation sentiment
Regional currency symbols and coin pile aesthetic communicate localized economic identity and player achievement within cultural context
Transition from accessible 'GET OUT OF JAIL' menu option to invisible/unavailable state when player is free communicates legal status change

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Portrait animates between neutral, happy, and angry faces on Trade screen; visual expression indicates acceptance likelihood and property valuation
Player eliminated from game display; properties revert to available/bank; visual removal from turn order
Menu displays 'GET OUT OF JAIL' option; movement restricted to jail square position; unique voice clip plays (regional variant: 'Don't be coming back, now' vs Japanese 'Whew, I finally got out')
Cash display reaches zero; properties transferred; character eliminated from board visibility and turn sequence
Uses original English price values despite 20x visual multiplier; creates exploitable trade imbalance where AI significantly undervalues properties

notes: Monopoly NES uses abstract representation model typical of board game digitization. World-state communication relies on property ownership coloring, cash display numerics, and UI status indicators rather than environmental hazards. The game's political/economic dimension manifests through resource scarcity visualization and player elimination mechanics. Regional localization includes significant UI/behavioral changes (Japanese animated NPC portraits, currency multipliers affecting AI trading logic). Voice acting localization reinforces cultural identity and emotional state communication. The 'YOU'RE NOT IN JAIL' unused text suggests comprehensive state tracking infrastructure even for edge cases.
